Build the rhythm with one-drop drums with soft snare, brushed hats, shakers, and tasteful fills; use smooth bass that supports soul chord changes. Feature electric piano, organ bubble, clean guitar, muted horns, and close vocal harmonies; use a warm soulful lead vocal. Keep melodies, riddims, horn lines, and basslines newly composed, with no named performer or existing song references. Arrange short bass intro, full groove, verse, chorus, stripped middle, dubby rebuild, final chorus, and hard stop. Shape it for sound-system low-end focus, strong low-end pocket, clear offbeat skank, tasteful delay and reverb, and no borrowed melody. Mix with separated kick, bass, guitar, keys, horns, vocals, and dub effects.
exclude: specific artist references, copyrighted melodies, copied riddims, existing song titles, famous reggae hooks, label references, recognizable samples, fake patois caricature, muddy sub bass, harsh clipping, thin snare
vocal: a warm soulful lead vocal
